original_abstract: Ensuing from the argument of Romans 7:7–25, 8:3–4 presents ‘the incapability of the law’ as a significant part of the greater problem of sin that needed resolution. This is brought to the fore best when the opening clause of Romans 8:3 (τὸ ... ἀδύνατον) is seen as an accusative of respect with dependant clauses and not as an anacoluthon as is often supposed. This opening clause points out sin’s two concomitant problems as (within this context) the primary points of reference in regard to which the claims of Romans 8:3–4 are made. Consequently, in Romans 8:3–4 Paul addresses the resolution of this threefold problem. In Romans 8:3 in particular, he argues that, through the mission of God’s Son, the problems of sin and the weakness of the flesh are resolved by the condemnation of sin in the flesh (of Christ). This has the purpose-result of also resolving the problem of the incapability of the law through Christ’s fulfilment of the requirement of the law (Rm 8:4).Romeine 8:3–4 en God se oplossing van die drievoudige probleem van sonde, die onvermoë van die wet en die swakheid van die vlees. In die lig van Romeine 7:7–25 impliseer 8:3–4 dat die ‘onvermoë van die wet’ ‘n noemenswaardige deel van die groter probleem van die sonde was wat opgelos moes word. Hierdie insig tree veral duidelik na vore wanneer die sinsnede waarmee Romeine 8:3 begin (τὸ ... ἀδύνατον) geïdentifiseer word as ‘n akkusatief van opsig (met bysinne) en nie, soos dikwels veronderstel word, ‘n anakoluthon nie. Die twee probleme wat met sonde gepaard gaan (die onvermoë van die wet en die swakheid van die vlees), word dus deur Paulus uitgelig as die primêre verwysingspunte ten opsigte waarvan die stellings in Romeine 8:3–4 gemaak word. In Romeine 8:3 wys Paulus dat God, deur sy Seun te stuur, die probleme van die sonde en die vlees aangespreek het deurdat die sonde in die vlees (van Christus) veroordeel is. Dit het die doel-gevolg gehad dat die meegaande probleem van die onvermoë van die wet ook opgelos is, aangesien Christus die vereiste van die wet vervul het (Rom 8:4).
humanized_abstract: Coming from the argument in Romans 7:7–25, Romans 8:3–4 says that the law cannot solve the problem of sin. The law’s failure is a big part of the larger problem of sin that needed a fix. I see the point most clearly when the opening clause of Romans 8:3 (τὸ ... ἀδύνατον) is read as a case of respect with extra clauses, not as a broken sentence that many think. I notice that the opening clause points out that sin has two problems. Those two problems are the key points that the writer of Romans 8:3–4 refers to. Romans 8:3–4 shows how Paul deals with a three part problem. In Romans 8:3, Paul says that the work of God’s Son solves the problem of sin and solves the problem of weak flesh. Paul says that the judgment of sin in the flesh of Christ ends the problem of sin and ends the problem of weak flesh. The purpose is to solve the problem of the law’s inability by having Christ fulfill the law’s requirement (Rm 8:4). Romeine 8:3–4 and God’s solution to the three‑part problem of sin, the law’s inability and the flesh’s weakness. In the light of Romeine 7:7–25, Romeine 8:3–4 shows that the law’s inability was a major part of the larger problem of sin needed solving. I see that the law’s inability and the flesh’s weakness together keep people trapped in sin, and that Christ’s work opens a way out. Ek kry insig wanneer ek die sin wat Romeine 8:3 begin, die Griekse frase τὸ ... ἀδύνατον, as ‘n akkusatief van opsig herken. Ek sien die sin nie as ‘n anakoluthon nie, soos sommige mense dink. Paul wys twee probleme wat met sonde kom. Die eerste probleem is die wet wat kan nie help nie. Die tweede probleem is die vlees wat is swak. Paul gebruik hierdie twee probleme as die belangrikste punte. Paul bou die uitsprake in Romeine 8:3–4 op hierdie twee probleme. In Romeine 8:3 wys Paulus dat God, deur sy Seun te stuur, die probleme van die sonde en die vlees aangespreek het deurdat die sonde in die vlees (van Christus) veroordeel is. Dit het die doel-gevolg gehad dat die meegaande probleem van die onvermoë van die wet ook opgelos is, aangesien Christus die vereiste van die wet vervul het (Rom 8:4).

Paul says that the judgment of sin in the flesh of Christ ends the problem of sin and ends the problem of weak flesh. Romeine 8:3–4 and God’s solution to the three‑part problem of sin, the law’s inability and the flesh’s weakness. In the light of Romeine 7:7–25, Romeine 8:3–4 shows that the law’s inability was a major part of the larger problem of sin needed solving. Paul wys twee probleme wat met sonde kom. Die eerste probleem is die wet wat kan nie help nie. Die tweede probleem is die vlees wat is swak.
